title: Mineral Wool Calculator
shortDescription: Work out the mineral wool needed for an attic, wall, or ceiling, including packs or rolls.
metaDescription: Estimate mineral wool volume, waste allowance, and the number of packs or rolls for attic, wall, or ceiling insulation.
intro: Enter the insulated area, thickness, waste allowance, and pack coverage to get the mineral wool volume and pack count.
overview:
Plan insulation with a result based on real dimensions rather than guesswork. The calculator converts area and thickness into the total wool volume, then uses the selected pack volume and coverage to estimate how many packs or rolls to buy.

It works well for pitched roofs, lofts, partition walls, and ceilings where the layer thickness matters. If you already know the product coverage from the supplier, enter it together with a sensible waste margin so the order matches the job on site.
howItWorks: Enter the insulated area in square meters.
Add the insulation thickness in centimeters.
Enter the coverage or area covered by one pack or roll.
Set the waste percentage for cutting and fitting losses.
Provide the pack volume to calculate the total material volume and package count.

useCases.title: Loft insulation order
useCases.description: Estimate the number of mineral wool packs for a pitched loft after measuring each insulated section.

useCases.title: Wall cavity planning
useCases.description: Match the wall area and insulation depth to the pack size before buying materials for internal lining.

useCases.title: Ceiling retrofit
useCases.description: Work out how much wool is required for a suspended ceiling or flat ceiling layer with a defined thickness.

useCases.title: Allowance for offcuts
useCases.description: Add a realistic margin for trimmings around rafters, joists, and edges.

useCases.title: Supplier pack comparison
useCases.description: Compare products with different coverage and pack volumes to choose the more practical order size.

examples.title: Loft conversion example
examples.input: Area: 84 m², Thickness: 22 cm, Coverage per pack: 6.4 m², Waste: 12%, Pack volume: 1.15 m³
examples.output: Returns the total wool volume and a pack count rounded up for the full order.
examples.description: Useful when ordering insulation for a loft conversion with several slopes and corners.

examples.title: Internal wall example
examples.input: Area: 31.5 m², Thickness: 12 cm, Coverage per pack: 4.8 m², Waste: 7%, Pack volume: 0.72 m³
examples.output: Shows the material volume, the adjusted coverage, and the minimum number of packs required.
examples.description: Helpful for a single room wall lining where the insulation depth is fixed.

examples.title: Small ceiling job
examples.input: Area: 18 m², Thickness: 10 cm, Coverage per roll: 3.6 m², Waste: 5%, Pack volume: 0.36 m³
examples.output: Calculates the needed volume and indicates how many rolls should be purchased.
examples.description: Suitable for a compact ceiling section where one roll size is specified on the label.

faq.question: What does the calculated volume mean?
faq.answer: It is the physical insulation volume needed for the measured area and thickness. Use it to compare against the volume listed on the product pack.

faq.question: Should I enter the finished thickness or the nominal product thickness?
faq.answer: Enter the thickness you want to achieve in the construction layer. If the product compresses or is installed in two layers, account for that in your planning.

faq.question: Why does the result round up the pack count?
faq.answer: You cannot buy part of a pack in practice, so the calculator rounds up to the next whole pack or roll.

faq.question: What if the pack coverage and pack volume do not match the same product data?
faq.answer: Use the figures from one product specification sheet. Mixing values from different products can produce an unrealistic order quantity.

faq.question: Can I use the result for double-layer insulation?
faq.answer: Yes, but calculate each layer separately if the thickness or product type changes. That gives a more reliable total than combining different layers into one entry.

seo.title: Mineral Wool Calculator
seo.description: Estimate mineral wool volume and pack count for loft, wall, or ceiling insulation using area, thickness, and waste allowance.
